name: Pagination
description: Navigation component for large datasets split across sequential pages. Supports full range display, compact variants, and configurable input controls for jumping to specific pages.
import: import { Pagination, Paginator, CompactPaginator, CompactInput, GoToInput } from "@salt-ds/core";

Pagination.description: Root context provider managing pagination state and page change callbacks. Wraps Paginator/CompactPaginator and optional input components.

count.description: Total number of pages. Determines page range displayed.

page.description: Currently active page (1-indexed). Controlled mode when provided.

defaultPage.description: Initial active page in uncontrolled mode. Defaults to 1.

onPageChange.description: Callback fired when active page changes (button click, input submission, keyboard).

Paginator.description: Full pagination display rendering all available page numbers (with truncation ellipsis if constrained). Default variant for typical use cases.

boundaryCount.description: Number of pages displayed adjacent to Previous/Next arrow buttons (minimum 1). Controls visible pages at range edges.

siblingCount.description: Number of page buttons displayed on each side of active page. Always configure in pairs for visual balance (1, 3, 5 etc).

CompactPaginator.description: Minimal pagination display showing only active page and last page. Renders Previous/Next navigation buttons. Ideal for space-constrained layouts.

GoToInput.description: Labeled input allowing users to jump directly to a specific page number. Includes label and integrates with Pagination state.

CompactInput.description: Minimal input variant without label. Displays page input only. Used with CompactPaginator for compressed layouts.

variant.description: Visual styling variant for input field.

pagination_size: Default (Paginator - full range with optional ellipsis)
states.page_selection: User navigates via Previous/Next buttons, page buttons, or input submission
states.truncation: Ellipsis displayed when page range exceeds available space (configurable via boundaryCount/siblingCount)
states.disabled: Previous button disabled on page 1; Next button disabled on final page

focusManagement.onLoad: Focus initially at first focusable element: input field (if left-positioned), Previous button (if on page 1), or first page (if active > 1).
focusManagement.focusTrap: Focus remains within pagination; Tab/Shift+Tab navigate buttons/input sequentially.

keyboard.Tab: Moves focus to next focusable element. From last element, focus exits pagination to next page element.
keyboard.Shift+Tab: Moves focus to previous focusable element. From first element, focus returns to prior page element.
keyboard.Enter: Activates focused page button or submits input value to navigate.
keyboard.Space: Activates focused button (same as Enter for button semantics).

aria: aria-label: set on Pagination root for screen reader context (e.g., 'Pagination Navigation')
aria-current="page": set on active page button to announce current location
aria-disabled: set on Previous btn (page 1) and Next btn (last page) when inactive
role="button": set on page number items for button semantics
Input fields use standard input accessibility (label, aria-label if needed)
accessibility.screenReader: Active page button announced with aria-current. Button/Page disable state announced. Input field submission triggers page change announcement via useAriaAnnouncer.

with: Text (current page, total pages in Compact variant)
positioning: Above + below content: recommended for long/multi-page content
composition.nesting: Do not nest Pagination inside Pagination. Use single root with Paginator/CompactPaginator variant.

patterns.with_goto_input: Pagination > { Paginator, GoToInput (labeled input for specific page) }
patterns.responsive: Combine Paginator at breakpoints ≥ lg, CompactPaginator at < lg
